_meta.description: Curated public comps for Australian renewable energy deals & benchmarks, ~2021-2026. Every row carries a source name + URL. Loaded by scripts/scrape-comps.js and used as the auto-seed for /comps (lib/compsDb.js).
_meta.scope: Australia. M&A/transaction valuations are public. Per-project cost-stack detail (AC/DC/EPC splits, system strength, land, rent, MLF) is largely confidential — those rows are either CSIRO GenCost benchmarks, the NSW Benefit Sharing Guideline, or clearly flagged 'Illustrative'.
_meta.disclaimer: Figures compiled from public reporting and may be approximate, rounded, or blended (e.g. EV/MW across operating + pipeline). Verify against primary documents before relying on them. Confidence flags: High = primary/authoritative source; Medium = reputable secondary; Low = derived/blended; Illustrative = placeholder archetype.
_meta.date_added_note: date_added = when the comp was added to this dataset (provenance; stable across DB resyncs). Distinct from transaction_date (the deal date).
_meta.data_class_note: data_class = Real (actual deals/projects/awards) vs Benchmark (published reference: CSIRO GenCost, NSW Benefit Sharing) vs Illustrative (placeholder archetypes).

deals.name: HMC Capital / Neoen Victoria portfolio
deals.notes: 652 MW operating: Bulgana (224 MW wind), Numurkah (128 MW solar), Victorian Big Battery (300 MW/450 MWh), plus ~2.8 GW dev pipeline. Divested by Neoen to satisfy the ACCC during Brookfield's takeover. Completed 1 Aug 2025.

metrics.notes: $/MW (auto-derived) is blended across wind/solar/BESS and includes ~2.8 GW pipeline — overstated for operating-only.

deals.name: Brookfield / Neoen (take-private)
deals.notes: EUR 6.1bn equity value (100%) at EUR 39.85/share. ~53% block acquired Dec 2024; squeeze-out / take-private completed Mar 2025. Global platform incl. major AU assets (Hornsdale; VBB pre-divestment).

metrics.notes: EUR 6.1bn equity value ~= A$10bn. Global platform, not AU-only.

deals.name: Sembcorp Industries / Alinta Energy
deals.notes: A$6.5bn enterprise value (US$4.32bn). 3.4 GW operational (gas, wind, solar, coal) + 10.4 GW pipeline. FIRB + ACCC approved. Announced 11 Dec 2025.

metrics.notes: $/MW (auto-derived) is across 3.4 GW incl. thermal — directional only, not RE-pure.

deals.name: La Caisse (CDPQ) / Edify Energy
deals.notes: ~C$1bn (~A$1.1bn / US$725m) incl. growth equity. Funds two ready-to-build solar+battery projects (900 MW/3,600 MWh; offtake to Rio Tinto + Federal Govt) and an 11 GW+ pipeline.

metrics.notes: C$1bn total incl. development equity, not a clean asset EV.

deals.name: Squadron Energy / CWP Renewables
deals.notes: A$4bn. ~1.1 GW operating wind (Sapphire, Murra Warra I & II) + ~20 GW pipeline. One of the largest AU renewables transactions to date.

metrics.notes: $/MW (auto-derived) on 1.1 GW operating is heavily overstated — also buys a ~20 GW pipeline.

deals.name: PowAR / Tilt Renewables (AU assets)
deals.counterparty: Powering Australian Renewables (AGL 20%, QIC 40%, Future Fund 40%)
deals.notes: Total scheme ~A$2.75bn (NZ$3.1bn EV). PowAR took the AU assets; Mercury NZ took the NZ assets. Tilt operated 836 MW across 7 wind farms (AU+NZ) + pipeline.

metrics.notes: Whole-of-scheme incl. NZ assets sold to Mercury; AU-only value is lower.

deals.name: KKR / OTPP / PSP — Spark Infrastructure
deals.notes: ~A$5.2bn all-cash. Spark holds stakes in TransGrid, SA Power Networks and Victoria Power Networks — grid infrastructure enabling the RE transition (network, not generation). Included as an adjacent-infra comp.

metrics.notes: Electricity networks platform, not generation capacity.

deals.name: J-Power / Genex Power
deals.notes: A$381m (A$0.275/share). ~400 MW committed (incl. Kidston pumped hydro 250 MW) + ~2.3 GW pipeline.

metrics.notes: $/MW (auto-derived) on 400 MW; mix of pumped hydro + solar + BESS, plus pipeline.

deals.name: Waratah Super Battery (Akaysha / BlackRock)
deals.notes: ~A$700m cost cap. EnergyCo SIPS 'shock absorber' for the NSW grid. First 350 MW online Aug 2025; full 850 MW expected 2026. ~2h duration.

metrics.notes: Publicly cited cost cap; auto-normalises to ~A$0.82m/MW and ~A$417k/MWh.

deals.name: Victorian Big Battery (Neoen / Tesla)
deals.notes: ~A$160m project (CEFC provided A$160m finance). 212 Tesla Megapacks. 2021 vintage — useful as an early build-cost datapoint vs. 2024-25 GenCost.

deals.name: Orana BESS (Akaysha / BlackRock)
deals.notes: A$650m debt financing (then the largest BESS financing globally), from 11 banks mid-2024. Total project cost not publicly disclosed. 448 Tesla Megapacks, 4h duration.

metrics.notes: Debt only (11-bank syndicate). Auto-normalises to ~A$391k/MWh of debt.

deals.name: Quinbrook Supernode BESS (Stages 1-2)
deals.notes: A$722m debt close (Jan 2025) — Australia's largest standalone BESS project financing to date. Stages 1+2 = 520 MW/1,856 MWh. Offtake to Origin Energy. ~3.6h duration.

deals.name: Akaysha QLD BESS (Brendale + Ulinda Park)
deals.notes: A$250m debt (7-bank syndicate) across two QLD projects totalling 710 MWh. Combined-project figure, so per-unit is blended.

deals.name: Golden Plains Wind Farm (TagEnergy)
deals.notes: ~A$4bn total project. 1,333 MW (756 MW Stage 1 East + 577 MW Stage 2 West) — largest wind farm in the Southern Hemisphere. Full completion mid-2027.

metrics.notes: CEFC commitment only ($222.5m Stage 1 + $127.5m Stage 2); full bank syndicate is larger and undisclosed.

deals.name: Western Downs Green Power Hub — Solar (Neoen)
deals.notes: ~A$600m total project cost for the 460 MWp solar farm (financed 2020, 7-lender syndicate). Among Australia's largest operating solar farms. 2020 vintage build-cost datapoint.

deals.name: New England Solar (ACEN)
deals.notes: 720 MW solar (Stage 1 400 MW operating since 2023; Stage 2 320 MW + 200 MW/400 MWh BESS) on a ~2,000 ha site near Uralla. ACEN raised A$750m portfolio debt + A$150m green term loan.

metrics.notes: ~2.8 ha/MW — real land-footprint comp for utility solar.

deals.name: Eraring Battery (Origin Energy)
deals.notes: Largest battery in the Southern Hemisphere: 700 MW/3,160 MWh across stages (~4.5h avg). Built on the retiring Eraring coal site. Stage 4 (Wartsila/Enerven) +A$80m. No clean total capex disclosed.

deals.name: Goyder South Stage 1 Wind (Neoen)
deals.notes: 412 MW (75 turbines), SA's biggest wind farm. Debt-financed in two tranches (209 MW + 203 MW). Underpinned by a 14-yr 100 MW PPA with the ACT Govt and a 10-yr 40 MW PPA with Flow Power; powers BHP's copper operations.

metrics.notes: ACT Government 100 MW contract; Flow Power 40 MW is 10-yr.

deals.name: Iberdrola / Infigen Energy
deals.notes: A$893m (A$0.92/share). 670 MW wind + 268 MW firming (incl. 25 MW/52 MWh Tesla battery) + 246 MW PPA offtake + >1 GW pipeline. Iberdrola's platform entry into Australia (2020; just outside the 5-yr window but a key benchmark).

metrics.notes: $/MW (auto-derived) on 670 MW wind; also buys firming + pipeline.

deals.name: Brookfield consortium / AusNet Services
deals.notes: A$2.65/share. Victorian electricity transmission + electricity/gas distribution networks — grid infrastructure enabling the RE transition (not generation). Last listed AU electricity infra company to be privatised.

deals.name: Collie Battery Stage 1 (Neoen)
deals.notes: 219 MW/877 MWh (4h) in WA's SWIS. A$160m EPC contract (Perth contractor). Part of Neoen's ~A$1.1bn portfolio financing. Capacity contract pays ~A$591k/MW/yr on 300 MW (~A$354m total).

deals.name: Hornsdale Power Reserve (Neoen / Tesla)
deals.notes: Australia's first big battery. 150 MW/194 MWh after 2020 expansion (+50 MW/64.5 MWh cost ~A$82m/EUR53m; co-funded SA Govt A$15m + ARENA A$8m + CEFC A$50m loan). Original 100 MW/129 MWh ~A$90m (2017).

deals.name: Stockyard Hill Wind Farm (Goldwind)
deals.notes: 530 MW, VIC. ~A$700m construction (Goldwind self-supplied turbines — outlier-low $/MW). Goldwind bought the development from Origin for A$110m (2017). Origin PPA set a record-low price <A$60/MWh.

metrics.notes: ~A$1.32m/MW — implausibly low vs GenCost $3.2m/MW; Goldwind vertical integration, 2018 vintage.
Origin offtake <A$60/MWh (closer to $50) — record low at the time.

deals.name: CSIRO GenCost 2024-25 — Large-scale solar PV
deals.notes: Authoritative national capex benchmark. Solar -8% YoY (second consecutive year).

deals.name: CSIRO GenCost 2024-25 — Onshore wind
deals.notes: Authoritative national capex benchmark. Onshore wind +6% YoY (incl. work-camp costs).

deals.name: CSIRO GenCost 2024-25 — Battery storage 2h
deals.notes: 2-hour grid-scale battery. Large-scale battery capex -20% YoY.

deals.name: CSIRO GenCost 2024-25 — Battery storage 4h
deals.notes: 4-hour grid-scale battery.

deals.name: NSW Benefit Sharing Guideline (2024) — Solar
deals.notes: NSW standard community benefit rate, CPI-indexed, over project life.

deals.name: NSW Benefit Sharing Guideline (2024) — Wind
deals.notes: NSW standard community benefit rate, CPI-indexed, over project life.

deals.name: NSW Benefit Sharing Guideline (2024) — BESS
deals.notes: NSW standard community benefit rate for storage, CPI-indexed, over project life.

deals.name: HumeLink (Transgrid)
deals.notes: ~500 kV, ~365 km (Wagga–Bannaby–Maragle) connecting Snowy 2.0. AER approved $4.57bn (Aug 2024); est. ~$4.88bn. $2.9bn construction contracts (Acciona/GenusPlus; UGL/CPB). Australia's most expensive transmission project.

deals.name: Marinus Link (Stage 1)
deals.notes: 750 MW Tas–Vic HVDC interconnector; ~250 km subsea + ~90 km land cable. Stage 1 ~A$3.86bn; full project est. ~A$4.8bn (AER, Mar 2025). FID Aug 2025; complete ~2030.

deals.name: Snowy 2.0 (Snowy Hydro)
deals.notes: 2.2 GW / ~350 GWh (~160h) pumped hydro. 2023 reset to ~A$12bn; trending toward ~A$20bn (2025 reassessment). Long-duration economics: very low $/MWh, high $/MW.

deals.name: Borumba Pumped Hydro (Queensland Hydro)
deals.notes: 2 GW / 48 GWh (24h) pumped hydro near Imbil. Initial ~A$14.2bn; blown out to ~A$18bn (Dec 2024). Business case under review (mid-2026).

deals.name: Star of the South (offshore wind)
deals.source: Star of the South / Infrastructure Pipeline
deals.notes: Up to 2.2 GW in the Gippsland offshore wind zone. Estimated A$8–10bn. Feasibility licence May 2024; targeting first power before 2030. First-of-a-kind AU offshore wind cost benchmark.

deals.name: Potentia Energy / 1GW+ AU portfolio
deals.notes: >1 GW portfolio: ~700 MW operational wind/solar + ~430 MW late-stage dev, incl. stake in Bright Energy Investments (Synergy JV, WA). Deal value undisclosed; US$550m financing raised to expand.

deals.name: CIP Murchison Green Hydrogen
deals.notes: ~1.8 Mtpa green ammonia powered by ~6 GW onshore wind + solar (WA). Secured A$814m under the Federal Hydrogen Headstart program (Mar 2025). Wider-scope green-hydrogen datapoint.

deals.name: Akaysha Elaine BESS (Victoria)
deals.notes: 311 MW/1,244 MWh (4h). A$460m financing. 15-yr virtual tolling agreement with Snowy Hydro (220 MW contracted) — largest 4h virtual toll in the market.

metrics.notes: Snowy Hydro virtual tolling agreement (220 MW contracted).

deals.name: Rio Tinto–Windlab Bungaban Wind PPA
deals.notes: Australia's largest renewable PPA: Rio buys 80% of the planned 1.4 GW Bungaban wind project (Windlab) for its Gladstone operations. Price confidential; term + volume disclosed.

deals.name: Rio Tinto–Edify Smoky Creek Solar + BESS PPA
deals.notes: Rio buys 90% of power + battery output from Edify's 600 MW Smoky Creek & Guthrie's Gap solar (540 MW contracted) for Gladstone. Part of a 2.7 GW Gladstone renewables push. Price confidential.

deals.name: BHP Olympic Dam–Neoen 24/7 PPA
deals.notes: 100 MW 24/7 baseload for BHP's SA copper operations (incl. Olympic Dam) from Neoen's Goyder North wind + battery. Firm term + start date disclosed; price confidential.

deals.name: BHP–Southern Cross Energy (TransAlta) PPA
deals.notes: 15-yr PPA extension through 31 Dec 2038 adding solar/wind/storage (18.5 MW solar at Leinster/Mt Keith). Long industrial renewable PPA.

deals.name: CIS Tender 1 — NEM Generation (round)
deals.notes: 19 successful projects, 6.4 GW (8 hybrids add 3.6 GWh storage). First NEM generation tender; opened 31 May 2024. CIS = revenue underwriting (price floor/ceiling), strike prices not disclosed.

deals.name: CIS Tender 2 — WEM Dispatchable (round)
deals.notes: 4 projects, 654 MW / 2,595 MWh (WEM). Binding community commitments: $145m shared benefits + $41.5m First Nations initiatives.

deals.name: CIS Tender 3 — NEM Dispatchable (round)
deals.notes: 16 battery projects from 124 applicants, 4.13 GW / 15.37 GWh — Australia's largest battery tender to date. VIC led with 1.33 GW. Opened 13 Nov 2024.

deals.name: CIS Tender 4 — NEM Generation (round)
deals.notes: 20 projects, 6.6 GW generation; co-located hybrids add 11.4 GWh (3.5 GW) of storage.

deals.name: CIS Tender 5 — WEM Generation (round)
deals.notes: 7 projects, 1.9 GW renewable generation (WEM/SWIS). Awarded alongside Tender 6.

deals.name: CIS Tender 6 — WEM Dispatchable (round)
deals.notes: 3 battery projects, 482 MW / 3,683 MWh — long durations (~6.9–8h) for WA's coal exit by 2030.

deals.name: CIS Tender 7 — NEM Generation (round)
deals.notes: 19 projects, 7.8 GW — largest CIS generation tender to date.

deals.name: Akaysha Deer Park BESS
deals.notes: 275 MW/1,100 MWh (4h) BESS in Victoria; awarded a CIS Tender 3 contract.

deals.name: Akaysha Ulinda Park BESS Stage 2
deals.notes: 195 MW/780 MWh (4h) BESS in Queensland; awarded a CIS Tender 3 contract.

deals.name: ACEN Birriwa Solar + BESS
deals.notes: 600 MW solar + 2.4 GWh BESS in the NSW Central-West Orana REZ; CIS Tender 4 hybrid.

deals.name: Trina Killawarra Solar-Battery Hybrid
deals.notes: 350 MW/2,100 MWh solar-battery hybrid — the sole hybrid under WEM Tender 5.

deals.name: Neoen Yathroo Wind Farm
deals.notes: 420 MW wind farm (WA); CIS Tender 5.

deals.name: Neoen Narrogin Wind Farm
deals.notes: 168 MW wind farm (WA); CIS Tender 5.

deals.name: Neoen Yathroo Battery
deals.notes: 200 MW/1,600 MWh (8h) battery (WA); CIS Tender 6.

deals.name: Collie Battery & Solar Hybrid (Enpowered/Plenary)
deals.notes: 200 MW/1,518 MWh (~7.6h) battery + solar hybrid at Collie (WA); CIS Tender 6.

deals.name: Frontier Waroona Renewable Energy Project Stage 1
deals.notes: 82 MW/565 MWh (~6.9h) at Waroona (WA); CIS Tender 6.

deals.name: NSW LTESA Tender 3 — Gen + LDS (round)
deals.notes: 5 projects: 750 MW generation (1 solar, 1 wind) + 524 MW/4,192 MWh long-duration storage (2 Li-ion BESS + 1 advanced-CAES). NSW Roadmap LTESA underwriting.

deals.name: NSW LTESA Tender 4 — Generation (round)
deals.notes: 2 generation projects, 312 MW: Maryvale Solar & Energy Storage and Flyers Creek wind farm.

deals.name: NSW LTESA Tender 5 — Long-Duration Storage (round)
deals.notes: Largest NSW LDS tender to date: 1.03 GW / 13.79 GWh across 3 projects (Stoney Creek BESS, Griffith BESS, ACEN Phoenix Pumped Hydro). All ≥8h dispatch.

deals.name: ACEN Phoenix Pumped Hydro
deals.notes: First pumped hydro project to win an NSW Roadmap LTESA (Tender 5); near Lake Burrendong. ≥8h dispatch.

deals.name: SA FERM Tender 1 (round)
deals.notes: First SA Firm Energy Reliability Mechanism tender: 6 projects, 1,334 MW / 5,336 MWh — all lithium-ion BESS (technology-neutral process, all-battery outcome). Supports SA's net 100% renewables by 2027.

deals.name: Neoen Goyder Battery (SA FERM)
deals.notes: Two FERMA contracts (Stage 1 + 2), 400 MW/1,600 MWh nameplate; 150 MW/1,200 MWh committed. Category 1 (Nov 2028 delivery).

deals.name: Ampyr Northern Battery (SA FERM)
deals.notes: 270 MW/1,080 MWh nameplate; 125 MW/1,000 MWh committed. Category 1 (Nov 2028).

deals.name: Iberdrola Tungkillo BESS (SA FERM)
deals.notes: 270 MW/1,080 MWh nameplate; 100 MW/800 MWh committed. Category 1 (Nov 2028).

deals.name: Akaysha Brinkworth BESS (SA FERM)
deals.notes: 250 MW/1,000 MWh nameplate; 92 MW/736 MWh committed. Category 2 (Nov 2029).

deals.name: ZEBRE Dartmoor BESS (SA FERM)
deals.notes: 144 MW/576 MWh nameplate; 50 MW/400 MWh committed. Category 2 (Nov 2029).

deals.name: VIC VRET auctions (round)
deals.notes: Victorian Renewable Energy Target support auctions (VRET1 2018, VRET2 2022). Delivered projects incl. Berrybank & Dundonnell wind; Winton (99 MW), Glenrowan (102 MW), Cohuna (34 MW) solar; Bulgana Green Power Hub. Underpins SEC's 100% renewable retail.

deals.name: SEC Melbourne Renewable Energy Hub (Melton)
deals.notes: SEC-backed hub near Melton: 1,200 MW/2,400 MWh BESS + 12.5 MW solar. Part of SEC's first 819 MW solar+storage tranche.

deals.name: SEC Renewable Energy Park (Horsham)
deals.notes: 119 MW solar + 100 MW/200 MWh BESS near Horsham. Part of SEC's first 819 MW solar+storage tranche.

deals.name: CleanCo Swanbank BESS
deals.notes: 250 MW/500 MWh (2h) publicly owned battery (Tesla/Yurika) at the Swanbank Clean Energy Hub; grid-connected Nov 2025.

deals.name: Central-West Orana REZ (access scheme)
deals.notes: NSW's first REZ; network build cost ~A$5.5bn (up from ~A$650m), recovered by ACEREZ over 35 yrs. Access-right holders pay an annual access fee funding hub-to-project (H2P) + centralised system strength (CSS) assets + community benefit.

metrics.notes: REZ network cost recovered via access fees + consumers.

deals.name: Blackstone + CPP / AirTrunk
deals.notes: A$24bn EV — largest-ever data-centre deal globally and Australia's biggest 2024 transaction. APAC platform (AU/JP/MY/HK/SG). 800 MW+ committed load + land for 1 GW+. EV/MW ~A$30m per MW of load.

metrics.notes: Committed to customers; 1 GW+ future land bank.

deals.name: CDC Data Centres (Infratil)
deals.notes: Independent valuation A$15.0bn (Mar 2026; range A$14.1–16.0bn). 568 MW installed across AU/NZ; >1 GW contracted (incl. a 555 MW US-customer contract — largest in AU history); target 1,820 MW by 2034.

metrics.notes: Independent valuation midpoint; EV/MW ~A$26m per MW installed.
>1 GW contracted incl. 555 MW US customer.

deals.name: NEXTDC (platform)
deals.notes: Listed AU data-centre operator. Pro-forma contracted utilisation ~667 MW (Mar 2026), up from ~228 MW end-2024 on AI demand. Raised ~A$2.2bn equity to fund rollout.

deals.name: AWS Australia data-centre investment
deals.notes: A$20bn capex commitment (2025–2029) to expand AU data-centre/cloud infrastructure (Sydney + Melbourne) — up from A$13.2bn (2023–2027). Largest tech-infrastructure investment in AU history; major new load driver.

metrics.notes: Multi-year capex commitment 2025–2029 (not a single asset).

deals.name: Goodman Group data-centre pipeline
deals.notes: Global data-centre 'power bank' reached 6.4 GW (3.6 GW secured + 2.8 GW in advanced procurement). Data centres ~73% of WIP; WIP ~A$18bn by Jun 2026. Global, AU-headquartered.

metrics.notes: Global power bank (6.4 GW); 3.6 GW secured.

deals.name: Utility solar — NSW ~200 MWac (illustrative archetype)
deals.notes: Placeholder cost-stack for items not publicly disclosed per project (AC/DC/EPC split, connection, system strength, land, MLF). Replace with real deal data as curated.

deals.name: Onshore wind — VIC ~300 MW (illustrative archetype)
deals.notes: Placeholder cost-stack. Capex benchmark also covered authoritatively by GenCost onshore wind.

deals.name: Standalone BESS — QLD 100 MW / 200 MWh (illustrative archetype)
deals.notes: Placeholder for cost-stack items not in GenCost (EPC %, connection, system strength). Capex benchmark covered by GenCost 2h battery.

deals.name: Palisade (Intera) / Limestone Coast North BESS
deals.notes: A$460m enterprise value (US$293m) for a 250 MW/500 MWh BESS in SA's Limestone Coast region. First large-scale BESS acquisition for Palisade/Intera. Phase 1 of Pacific Green's 500MW/1.5GWh Limestone Coast Energy Park. Expected operational by early 2027.

metrics.notes: A$460m EV at financial close, Feb 2025. ~A$1.84m/MW and ~A$920k/MWh.

deals.name: KKR / Zenith Energy (Australia)
deals.seller: Pacific Equity Partners, OPTrust, Foresight Group
deals.notes: A$1.7bn (USD ~1.1bn) deal for Australia's leading off-grid IPP. ~710 MW contracted capacity across ~15 remote/microgrid sites in WA and NT. Zenith completed A$1.9bn debt refinancing. Founder/management retain minority. Close expected late 2025. EV not officially confirmed — Bloomberg/media figure.

metrics.notes: A$1.7bn — media-reported figure (Bloomberg); not officially disclosed by KKR. ~A$2.4m/MW on 710 MW.

deals.name: Clarke Creek Wind Farm Stage 2 (Squadron Energy)
deals.notes: A$1bn debt facility (10-bank syndicate, financial close 4 Dec 2025) for Stage 2 of Clarke Creek Wind Farm (~450 MW). Combined Stages 1+2 exceed 1 GW — largest single-asset wind financing in Australian market history. Stage 1 (also ~450 MW) already operating. No capex or PPA price disclosed.

metrics.notes: A$1bn from 10 banks — record single-asset wind financing in Australia. ~A$2.2m/MW of debt.
CS Energy 20-yr PPA on Stage 1 Boulder Creek (comparable project structure); Clarke Creek offtake terms not publicly disclosed.

deals.name: Potentia Energy portfolio financing (Enel/Inpex)
deals.notes: A$830m portfolio debt from 7 banks (Bank of China, BNP Paribas, HSBC, Mizuho, SocGen, SMBC, Westpac) secured Dec 2025. Covers 800+ MW of existing wind/solar/hybrid assets (Bungala 275MW, Clare 128MW, Girgarre 93MW, Cohuna 34MW, Royalla 23MW, Greenough River 40MW, Quorn Park 98MW + BESS). National portfolio — individual per-MW benchmarks are blended.

metrics.notes: Portfolio debt facility across 800+ MW operating assets. ~A$1.04m/MW blended debt (solar-heavy).

deals.name: Boulder Creek Wind Farm (Aula / CS Energy)
deals.notes: ~A$740m total project cost (38 GE Vernova turbines, 228 MW), ~40 km SW of Rockhampton QLD. Financial close 27 Sep 2024. CS Energy holds 100% PPA (20-yr); JV 50/50 ownership. Debt from CBA, NAB, ANZ, ING. Construction began Nov 2024; commercial operations 2027. Aula Energy is a Macquarie-backed vehicle.

metrics.notes: CS Energy 20-yr PPA for 100% of output.

deals.name: Palmer Wind Farm (Tilt Renewables)
deals.notes: 288 MW SA wind farm (40 Vestas V172-7.2 MW turbines — most powerful in AU), ~70 km east of Adelaide. FID/financial close 31 Dec 2025. CIS Tender 4 award. AGL long-term PPA. Construction mid-2026; commissioning late 2027. Total project cost not disclosed; part of Tilt's A$2.25bn corporate refinancing.

metrics.notes: AGL PPA — 'long-term'; 15yr is estimated comparable to Waddi (same developer). Term not publicly disclosed.

deals.name: Waddi Wind Farm (Tilt Renewables)
deals.notes: 108 MW WA wind farm (~150 km north of Perth), first wind FID in Australia in 2025. AGL 15-yr PPA. Vestas turbines. Construction 2026; operations 2028. Estimated capex ≥A$400m per media (US$265m). Part of Tilt's A$850m dual-tranche syndicated facility. Community benefit A$3.9m over life.

metrics.notes: ~A$400m media estimate (A$3.7m/MW); part of Tilt's A$850m portfolio facility — project-level capex not separately disclosed.
A$3.9m total over project life (~25yr); ~A$156k/yr average.

deals.name: Golden Plains BESS (TagEnergy)
deals.notes: 150 MW/600 MWh (4h) BESS at Golden Plains Wind Farm site, VIC. Financial close Dec 2025. Lenders: Westpac, Bank of China, Siemens Financial Services. Long-term virtual tolling agreement with Snowy Hydro for most of capacity. Tesla 168 Megapack 2XL; EPC by CPP. Commissioning mid-2027. Project cost not disclosed.

metrics.notes: Snowy Hydro virtual tolling — term not disclosed. 15yr is estimate by analogy with Akaysha Elaine (same structure, same counterparty).

deals.name: NEXTDC S7 / OpenAI Eastern Creek campus
deals.notes: 550 MW sovereign AI campus at Eastern Creek, Western Sydney. A$7bn total investment. OpenAI is anchor offtaker under MOU (OpenAI for Countries program). NEXTDC acquired the site in 2024 for ~A$353m. First phase expected H2 2027 (subject to approvals). Closed-loop liquid cooling; powered by long-term renewable PPAs. Exceeds CDC's 504 MW facility as largest planned in Southern Hemisphere.

metrics.notes: A$7bn total investment — media-reported figure. Includes land (A$353m site) + construction.
550 MW IT load capacity per The Urban Developer.

deals.name: Microsoft Australia AI infrastructure commitment
deals.notes: A$25bn (USD$18bn) capital commitment to expand Australia's Azure AI cloud infrastructure by >140% by end-2029. Announced 23 Apr 2026 by Satya Nadella + PM Albanese. Covers cybersecurity, AI supercomputing and workforce training. Existing footprint 29 sites across 3 Azure regions. MW capacity not disclosed — capex-only benchmark for hyperscaler infra scale in AU.

metrics.notes: Multi-year capex 2026–2029; not a single-asset figure. Useful as a hyperscaler-commitment scale benchmark alongside AWS A$20bn.

deals.name: Partners Group / GreenSquareDC
deals.notes: Partners Group to invest up to AUD 1.2bn to transform GreenSquareDC into a hyperscale/AI data centre platform across Sydney, Melbourne and Perth. First Sydney site is a brownfield with powered shell + adjoining landbank enabling expansion to 96 MW of compute capacity. IT load (96 MW) is the disclosed site expansion capacity, not platform-wide; investment is a platform commitment not a single-asset EV, so $/MW from these two figures is indicative only.

deals.name: AirTrunk MEL2 (Melbourne campus)
deals.notes: AirTrunk's second Melbourne hyperscale campus (MEL2) in Melbourne's north-west: 354 MW IT load, more than A$5bn new direct investment. Lifts AirTrunk's total deployable Melbourne capacity to over 630 MW (MEL1+MEL2 combined >A$7bn). IT load 354 MW. Capex/MW derivable from $5bn / 354 MW.

deals.name: Stockland / Fife Capital — Kemps Creek (Western Sydney)
deals.notes: Planning application filed for a 168 MW hyperscale data centre (twelve 14 MW data halls) at 90 Aldington Road, Kemps Creek, West Sydney; up to AU$3.94bn investment. 1 GW substation also planned to serve the wider industrial park. Direct-to-chip liquid cooling. IT load 168 MW.

deals.name: NEXTDC M4 (Fishermans Bend, Melbourne)
deals.notes: NEXTDC received Victorian Government development approval for the M4 technology campus at Fishermans Bend, Port Melbourne (former Westgate Park Printing Complex, 127 Todd Road). 162 MW, A$2bn. Direct-to-chip liquid cooling supporting 1000 kW rack densities. IT load 162 MW. Distinct from existing NEXTDC platform / NEXTDC S7 entries.

deals.name: NEXTDC Project Berlin — Western Sydney 850MW JV (S4/S7)
deals.notes: NEXTDC seeking a JV partner (Project Berlin; advisors Morgan Stanley + Cadence Advisory) for 850 MW of new data-centre capacity in Western Sydney (S4 + S7), AU$15bn over 10+ years. NEXTDC to retain minority stake and operate. Financial close targeted 12-18 months. IT load 850 MW (planned aggregate). Capex is a long-dated programme figure, not single-asset EV.

deals.name: Macquarie Data Centres — IC3 Super West (Macquarie Park, Sydney)
deals.notes: Macquarie Data Centres topped out IC3 Super West at its Macquarie Park (Sydney) campus: 47 MW total planned capacity (6 MW IT load fitted in Phase 1), ~A$350m investment, purpose-built for high-density AI / liquid cooling. Opening Q3 2026. IT load: 47 MW total planned; capacity_mw = 47 MW total, contracted/fitted Phase-1 load = 6 MW.

metrics.notes: Phase-1 investment only (core+shell with 6 MW fitted); not the full 47 MW build-out. Do not derive $/MW against 47 MW.
Total planned capacity; only 6 MW fitted in Phase 1 (see contracted_load).
